steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “even in escape contexts” signals that evidence must show escape—the highest-stakes scenario—as the case where animals avoid maximal speed. This proves energy costs are powerful enough to override survival pressure.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B — multiple lizard species move at an average of less than 90% of their maximal speed while escaping predation. directly demonstrates this: multiple lizard species move at less than 90% of maximal speed while escaping, showing that energy constraints are severe enough to restrict even life-or-death flight far below maximum.

The other choices conflate escape and pursuit, show animals using near-maximal speeds, or address the wrong context.

That matches Choice B — multiple lizard species move at an average of less than 90% of their maximal speed while escaping predation..
